commands.scenario: Check if a connection is open

commands.command: command.CommandTimeout = 60; // Sets the wait time (in seconds) before terminating an attempt to execute a command.
commands.description: Performance tuning: Adjusts the execution timeout to prevent long-running queries from hanging the application indefinitely.
Workflow pattern: Automatically closes the associated connection when the DataReader is closed, preventing connection leaks.
Advanced usage: Instructs the command object to treat the CommandText as a stored procedure name rather than a raw SQL string.

commands.command: SqlParameter param = new SqlParameter("@id", SqlDbType.Int); param.Value = 10; command.Parameters.Add(param);
commands.description: Performance & Security: Explicitly defining SqlDbType prevents implicit type conversion overhead and mitigates SQL injection more robustly than AddWithValue.
Performance tuning: Configures Connection Pooling settings in the connection string to optimize resource reuse and manage database load.
